bins.name: Reliability framework for flexible loads
bins.desc: NERC supports integrating large data center loads while maintaining reliability, informed by prior grid events where loads amplified instability. The organization commits to interim guidance and parallel standards development to ensure loads contribute reliably to system operations.
quotes: we must stay laser-focused on reliability, informed by NERC's analysis of grid events in Virginia and Texas, where large data center loads have responded to, and amplified, grid instability.
We understand we must quickly secure the reliable contribution of these loads to the future system, especially given the lessons learned related to the integration of inverter-based resources.
The Level 2 Alert report also underscored the need for a Level 3 Alert to urge essential actions and provide guidance while registry criteria and Reliability Standards are modified.

A Level 3 Alert and guidance materials throughout 2026 via NERC and its subgroups will ensure that risk mitigation starts right away, while the appropriate regulatory process works to deliver the necessary updates to registration and standards.
NERC is committed to identifying and mitigating risks to the BPS so that the grid continues to support the North American economy and innovation.

NERC, in partnership with the Regional Entities, is committed to doing our part as the Electric Reliability Organization to enable the rapid growth of these key loads in a manner that preserves—and potentially enhances—reliability for all.

bins.name: Standards and registry development
bins.desc: NERC is accelerating development of registry criteria and Reliability Standards specifically for large loads, targeting completion by December 31, 2026. These updates will provide clarity on which entities must comply and what actions they must take.
quotes: Under the accelerated large load action plan, NERC intends to file revised registry criteria and Reliability Standards on or before December 31, 2026.
NERC concluded that it would accelerate the large load action plan and initiate efforts to update its registry criteria and develop initial Reliability Standards to include large loads.

These updates would provide certainty on which parties should be subject to NERC requirements (per updated registry criteria) and what those parties should be required to do (per updated Reliability Standards).

bins.name: Unprecedented data center load growth
bins.desc: Large data center loads driven by artificial intelligence deployment represent unprecedented growth in recent memory, creating urgent demand for reliable grid integration.
quotes: We are entering a period of load growth unprecedented in recent memory, driven by data centers that support our daily lives and advance North America's leadership in the deployment of artificial intelligence.

bins.name: Reliable integration of large loads
bins.desc: NERC must secure reliable contribution of large loads to the system while applying lessons from prior resource integration challenges. The goal is to enable rapid data center growth while preserving or enhancing overall reliability.
quotes: We understand we must quickly secure the reliable contribution of these loads to the future system, especially given the lessons learned related to the integration of inverter-based resources.

NERC, in partnership with the Regional Entities, is committed to doing our part as the Electric Reliability Organization to enable the rapid growth of these key loads in a manner that preserves—and potentially enhances—reliability for all.

bins.name: Accelerated work schedule
bins.desc: NERC is processing large load integration tasks in parallel rather than sequentially to meet the urgent timeline, with standards and registry criteria to be delivered by year-end 2026.
quotes: As a result, NERC is currently processing many tasks in parallel to address large load integration, rather than in sequence as we normally would.
Under the accelerated large load action plan, NERC intends to file revised registry criteria and Reliability Standards on or before December 31, 2026.

bins.name: Interim guidance and alerts
bins.desc: NERC plans a Level 3 Alert in May 2026 and ongoing guidance materials throughout 2026 to urge essential interim actions and risk mitigation while registry and standards updates proceed through the regulatory process.
quotes: NERC also plans a Level 3 Alert in early May of 2026 to urge essential actions in the interim.
The Level 2 Alert report also underscored the need for a Level 3 Alert to urge essential actions and provide guidance while registry criteria and Reliability Standards are modified.

A Level 3 Alert and guidance materials throughout 2026 via NERC and its subgroups will ensure that risk mitigation starts right away, while the appropriate regulatory process works to deliver the necessary updates to registration and standards.

bins.name: Reliability Standards for large loads
bins.desc: NERC is developing initial Reliability Standards focused on essential actions for integrating large computational loads in the near term, with a Standard Authorization Request approved for posting in March 2026.
quotes: NERC concluded that it would accelerate the large load action plan and initiate efforts to update its registry criteria and develop initial Reliability Standards to include large loads.
